channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliament reimburses investigator R80,000 for initial two-day testimony before ad hoc committee
item.description: Private investigator Paul O’Sullivan revealed that Parliament paid him R80,000 for his first two-day appearance before an ad hoc committee investigating alleged corruption and political interference i...

item.title: Kenya Reports Increase in Influenza Cases, Ministry of Health Advises Preventive Measures
item.description: The Ministry of Health has raised alarm over a noticeable rise in influenza cases across multiple regions in Kenya. According to Dr Patrick Amoth, Health Director General, surveillance data from the K...

item.title: Performance of Dollar, Government Bonds and Gold as Safe Havens Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has once again pushed investors to look for safe places to park their money, but traditional safe havens are not behaving as expected this time. The US dollar h...

item.title: Paul O’Sullivan responds to ActionSA corruption claims, confirms past donations
item.description: Forensic investigator Paul O’Sullivan has publicly denied allegations of corruption linked to ActionSA, while acknowledging that he made donations to the party when it was first established. Speaking ...

item.title: Nova Scotia disability advocates criticise proposed budget cuts to support programs and caregiver benefits
item.description: Parents and advocates in Nova Scotia are expressing deep concern and feelings of betrayal over the Progressive Conservative government's proposed budget cuts, which disproportionately affect people wi...

item.title: Akor Adams Describes Ademola Lookman as One of the Quietest in Super Eagles Camp
item.description: Super Eagles striker Akor Adams recently shared insights about his teammate Ademola Lookman during an interview with Sporty TV. He pointed out that Lookman, along with Semi Ajayi, is among the quietes...

item.title: Albanese Government Backs US-Israeli Strikes on Iran Amid Political and Social Challenges
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese have expressed support for the recent US-Israeli strikes on Iran, citing concerns about nuclear threats and terrorism...

item.title: FG approves promotions for 70,000 paramilitary officers and strengthens immigration operations
item.description: The Federal Government (FG) don announce say over 70,000 paramilitary officers don get promotion for the past three years. Dr. Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, the Minister of Interior, yan this one for Abuja duri...

item.title: Pakistanis Escape Iran Amid Widespread Missile Strikes and Explosions
item.description: Hundreds of Pakistanis have fled Iran as the ongoing US-Israel war has escalated sharply, causing missile strikes and explosions across Tehran. Students, businessmen, and pilgrims reported buildings e...

item.title: Sauti za Busara Festival 2027 Opens Call for African Musicians Applications
item.description: The organisers of the famous Sauti za Busara music festival have started receiving applications for musicians to perform at the 2027 edition, which will happen in Stone Town, Zanzibar, from 11 to 14 M...

item.title: European countries deploy naval and air defences to support Cyprus after drone strike on UK base
item.description: Several European countries have moved to strengthen Cyprus’s defence following a drone attack on a British military base on the island, amid criticism that the United Kingdom’s naval response will tak...

item.title: Tanzania MPs prepare to review 2026/27 Sh61.9 trillion budget plan
item.description: Tanzania is entering an important period in its financial calendar as the government gets ready to present the proposed budget ceiling for the 2026/27 financial year. Parliamentary committee meetings ...

item.title: US Sinks Iranian Warship Near Sri Lanka Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: A US submarine has sunk the Iranian warship IRIS Dena in the Indian Ocean off Sri Lanka's southern coast, killing at least 87 crew members and leaving dozens more missing. The vessel was returning to ...

item.title: Showmax Announces Exit from Kenyan Market
item.description: Showmax, the video streaming service owned by MultiChoice and recently acquired by French media company Canal+, has announced plans to exit the Kenyan market. The company said the decision came after ...

item.title: Challenges Facing Women-Led Farming Cooperatives in South Africa Due to Failing Irrigation Systems
item.description: In KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa, women farmers have faced significant challenges due to broken and outdated irrigation infrastructure. The Isibonelo Cooperative, a small-scale women-led farming group w...

item.title: India Formally Condoles Death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Through Diplomatic Gesture
item.description: On 5 March 2026, India officially expressed condolences over the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in joint US-Israel airstrikes on Tehran a few days earlier. Forei...

item.title: Woman jailed for five years and four months over culpable homicide of Asda security guard in Arbroath shoplifting incident
item.description: A 38-year-old woman, Natasha Smith, has been sentenced to five years and four months in prison at the High Court in Edinburgh for the culpable homicide of Alun Harris-Richards, a 61-year-old former Ro...

item.title: Allergy awareness training and spare adrenaline injectors to become mandatory in English schools
item.description: The Department for Education has announced new statutory guidance making allergy awareness training compulsory for all staff in schools across England for the first time. Schools will also be required...

item.title: Alex Eala to Face Dayana Yastremska in Second Round of Indian Wells Open
item.description: Filipina tennis sensation Alex Eala is set to kick off her campaign at the prestigious BNP Paribas Open in Indian Wells, California, one of the biggest WTA 1000 tournaments, often called the 'Fifth Gr...

item.title: Escalating Conflict in the Middle East: Iran, Israel, and U.S. Military Strikes Intensify
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has escalated sharply, with Iran launching a series of attacks against Israel, U.S. military bases, and neighbouring countries. This follows the U.S. and Israel's initi...
